title: silene gynodioica subsp. glandulosa
intro: Silene gynodioica subsp. glandulosa, also known as the glandular twinflower or glandular silene, is a plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) characterized by its unique reproductive system, with separate male and female plants. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Silene gynodioica subsp. glandulosa include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to promote wound healing and alleviate respiratory issues.
